items.headline: Benge and Vientos lead the Mets to a victory over the skidding Rockies
items.description:
DENVER (AP) — Carson Benge homered leading off the sixth inning for New York’s first hit against Tomoyuki Sugano, and the Mets beat the skidding Colorado Rockies 4-2 on Monday in a game moved up three hours to avoid potential wet weather.
Mark Vientos added a two-run single for the Mets, who are 3-1 on a nine-game trip after losing 17 of 20 overall. Benge also saved a run with a diving catch in center field.

items.headline: Right-hander Lou Trivino agrees to 1-year contract with Orioles after release by Phillies
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Right-handed reliever Lou Trivino agreed to a one-year contract with the Baltimore Orioles on Monday, three days after he was released from a minor league deal by the Philadelphia Phillies.
The 34-year-old Trivino was 3-0 with a 2.77 ERA in 10 relief outings for the Triple-A Lehigh Valley IronPigs.

items.headline: White Sox sign veteran outfielder Randal Grichuk to $1.25 million deal; place Austin Hays back on IL
items.description:
ANAHEIM, Calif. (AP) — The Chicago White Sox signed veteran outfielder Randal Grichuk to a one-year, $1.25 million deal on Monday and placed outfielder Austin Hays back on the 10-day injured list because of a left calf strain.
Grichuk, who elected free agency after being designated for assignment by the New York Yankees last week, should give the White Sox a solid right-handed bat against left-handed pitchers.

items.headline: Giants call up top prospect Bryce Eldridge to try to jolt a sputtering offense
items.description:
SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The San Francisco Giants called up top prospect Bryce Eldridge on Monday to help boost their sputtering offense.
Eldridge was the designated hitter batting in the No. 7 slot in manager Tony Vitello’s lineup for Monday night’s game against the San Diego Padres.

items.headline: Astros scratch catcher Yainer Diaz with a left abdominal injury before start of Dodgers series
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — Catcher Yainer Diaz was scratched from Houston’s lineup about an hour before the start of a series against the Los Angeles Dodgers on Monday night because of a left abdominal injury.
Christian Vázquez took his spot in the lineup and was batting ninth.

items.headline: Demoted to minors, Anthony Volpe has to earn back New York Yankees shortstop job from José Caballero
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Anthony Volpe has to earn back his New York Yankees job, just like any other prospect.
New York optioned the Gold Glove-winning shortstop to Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre on Sunday following the end of a 20-day minor league injury rehabilitation assignment that followed Oct. 14 surgery to repair a torn labrum in his left shoulder.

items.headline: Marlins promote catcher Mack from Triple-A, demote struggling Ramírez to minors
items.description:
MIAMI (AP) — The Miami Marlins promoted highly-rated catcher Joe Mack from Triple-A and demoted slumping catcher Agustín Ramírez before the club’s game against the Philadelphia Phillies on Monday.
Mack, rated the fifth catching prospect in the major leagues and 54th fourth overall, started behind the plate and went 0 for 3 in Miami’s 1-0 loss.

items.headline: Mariners catcher Cal Raleigh sitting out a 3rd straight game due to a sore right side
items.description:
SEATTLE (AP) — Seattle Mariners catcher Cal Raleigh is out of the starting lineup for the third straight game with a sore right side, manager Dan Wilson said Monday.
Raleigh began experiencing soreness following Friday night’s game against the Kansas City Royals and was a late scratch on Saturday.

items.headline: Brewers' Jackson Chourio goes 4 for 4 in his 2026 debut after coming off injured list
items.description:
ST. LOUIS (AP) — Milwaukee’s Jackson Chourio produced a spectacular season debut in a losing cause Monday after missing the first month of the season.
Chourio went 4 of 4 and hit a pair of doubles in the Brewers’ 6-3 loss to the St. Louis Cardinals. Chourio and teammate Andrew Vaughn came off the injured list earlier in the day after both players were dealing with fractured bones in their left hand.

items.headline: Two-time AL Cy Young winner Tarik Skubal to have elbow surgery to remove loose bodies
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Two-time American League Cy Young Award winner Tarik Skubal will have surgery to remove loose bodies from his elbow after being scratched from Monday's start against the Boston Red Sox.
Detroit Tigers manager A.J. Hinch told reporters that the left-hander felt discomfort in his elbow on Sunday, less than a week after an injury scare against the Atlanta Braves. The 29-year-old — set to become a free agent next offseason — underwent tests that showed the loose bodies. The surgery hadn't yet been scheduled.
